ships.name: Blight Shadow
ships.description: An invasive species from beyond our galaxy. They primarily survive by absorbing various forms of energy.

ships.name: Blight Hatchling
ships.description: A recently hatched Blight Shadow.

ships.name: Blight Mutation
ships.description: An invasive species from beyond our galaxy. They primarily survive by absorbing various forms of energy. These Blight Shadows have mutated into a stronger form that includes an organ to protect itself from incoming projectiles.

ships.name: Blight Horror
ships.description: An invasive species from beyond our galaxy. They primarily survive by absorbing various forms of energy. The Blight Horror has developed a special organ which allows the creature to generate bolts of red lightning that can interfere with mechanical devices. Unlike the energy absorption and draining organs that are used to gain sustenance, the primary purpose of this attack appears to be malicious vindictive destruction.

ships.name: Blight Terror
ships.description: An invasive species from beyond our galaxy. They primarily survive by absorbing various forms of energy. A Blight Shadow that has gorged itself on an unusual source of energy can become a Blight Terror, growing to an absurd size.

ships.name: Osk Lek Chaser
ships.description: A heavily modified Korath Chaser used as an escape vehicle by the Scrapyard Hermit.

ships.name: Palavret Tek
ships.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships.

ships.name: Rano'erek Tek
ships.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships. 	This Rano'erek Tek has enhanced shield strength and hull durability. Upgraded automation in its operations keeps the ships crew requirements low.

ships.name: Rano'erek Ik
ships.description: The dangers of scavenging in the Scrapyard has lead the captain of this Rano'erek Ik to make some modifications to its hull with a focus on strengthening the shields and expanding weapons capacity.

ships.name: Mechanized Mine
ships.description: An automated mine designed to create more mines. The Mereti developed this for deployment in distant systems controlled by hostile forces.

ships.name: Grakerat 572
ships.description: The Sestor deployed these expeditionary vessels centuries ago to extend their reach beyond Korath space. If one were to find a suitable planet to establish a base of operations on, the Sestor could rise again.

Missions: Scrapyard Ae Il B-11 Expeditionary Fleet

ships.name: Skoretak 13
ships.description: The Sestor stopped production of these interceptors a century ago. Their primary function was to serve as escorts for freighters.

Missions: Scrapyard Ae Il B-11 Expeditionary Fleet

ships.name: Tercheldar 150
ships.description: The Tercheldar 150 was a warship designed to fight in Mereti mine fields. It was eventually replaced by the more powerful Kar Ik Vot 349.

Missions: Scrapyard Ae Il B-11 Expeditionary Fleet

ships.name: Stakoshtar 187
ships.description: During their civil war, the Korath used this weapon against planetary targets. Scars from such battles still cover the surface of many of their worlds.

ships.name: Eave-Skiff
ships.description: The Eave-Skiff is a fighter carried by Kor Efret capital ships. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

Missions: Midnight Expansion: Kor Efret Tevah-Ark Coracle

ships.name: Vault-Barge
ships.description: A precursor to the Korath World-Ship. As more of their people resettle onto their former worlds, there will be a demand for smaller ships. In anticpation of this, the Kor Efret shipyards have begun reproducing these smaller freighters.

ships.name: Bravo-Monitor
ships.description: A precursor to the Korath World-Ship. As more of their people resettle onto their former worlds, there will be a demand for smaller ships. In anticpation of this, the Kor Efret shipyards have begun reproducing these smaller patrol ships.

ships.name: Delta-Ferry
ships.description: A precursor to the Korath World-Ship. As more of their people resettle onto their former worlds, there will be a demand for smaller ships. In anticpation of this, the Kor Efret shipyards have begun reproducing these smaller transports.

ships.name: Korath Ranger
ships.description: The Threshold-Destroyer was the first warship deployed at the start of the Korath civil war that eventually brought the downfall of their civilization. With the recent Blight Shadow attack on one of their World-ships, the Kor Efret are starting to reproduce these warships as escorts.

ships.name: Shelf-Sloop
ships.description: The Kor Efret have redesigned an old warship for more peaceful pursuits. The Shelf-Sloop is able to install larger engines than its predecessor while also accomodating a turret. Its weapons options, however, are more limited.

ships.name: Mereti Efret
ships.description: Translated to "Mereti Friend", this is the first functional Korath spacecraft made entirely from salvaged Mereti parts. While not fully autonomous, it is very nearly so.

ships.name: Korath Devourer
ships.description: Some Korath scrappers are more interested in harvesting raw materials rather than technology. The Devar'ur is their attempt to streamline the collection process. The Korath Incinerator is integrated into the ship and cannot be removed.

ships.name: Korath Explorer
ships.description: Korath Explorers were originally made to escape the harsh gravitional forces of Sagittarius A*. It is a science vessel designed for gathering data and resources.

variants.name: Grakerat 572 (Infested)
variants.description: The Sestor deployed these expeditionary vessels centuries ago to extend their reach beyond Korath space. If one were to find a suitable planet to establish a base of operations on, the Sestor could rise again.

variants.name: Korath Devourer (Infested)
variants.description: Some Korath scrappers are more interested in harvesting raw materials rather than technology. The Devar'ur is their attempt to streamline the collection process. The Korath Incinerator is integrated into the ship and cannot be removed.

variants.name: Palavret (Korath Raider (Infested))
variants.description: Successive raider designs have been the Korath warship of choice for plundering neighboring species for generations. Named for a celebrated captain who gave her life to ensure her crippled vessel made it back to port with its desperately needed cargo of stolen fish, the Palavret-Class Raiding Ship continues that grim tradition. 	Like all Exile ships, the Palavret has been adapted to house as many Korath as possible, as comfortably as possible, inflating its size beyond similarly armed warships. The spaceborne construction techniques employed allow for the ship's easy replacement, facilitating continued raids of nearby systems.

variants.name: Rano'erek (Korath Dredger (Infested))
variants.description: After the Korath were exiled from their homelands, efficient resource collection became a priority. World-ships were too precious to risk on dangerous forays, and raiders lacked the cargo capacity. The Rano'erek-class Asteroid Dredging Ship, named for a respected civilian politician and economist, was born as an answer to this problem. 	Featuring an abundance of cargo and passenger space in addition to their formidable defensive armament, these bulky ships can also offer potent support for frontline Palavrets.

variants.name: Model 512 (Infested)
variants.description: This is the final state in the evolution of a Kor Mereti war drone: a perfect ring, with incredibly powerful shields and turrets firing in every direction.

variants.name: Model 256 (Infested)
variants.description: There is a saying among the Quarg that every species which insists on studying war will eventually construct a weapon that outlives its creators. For the Korath, their war drones have very nearly done so.

variants.name: Kar Ik Vot 349 (Infested)
variants.description: The largest and deadliest of the Kor Sestor robotic warships, a single one of these battlecruisers can hold its own against an entire fleet of lesser ships.

variants.name: Tek Far 71 - Lek (Infested)
variants.description: The Kor Sestor war fleets rely heavily on small, powerful fighters and drones. This ship can carry up to ten of their drones, clamped to the exterior of its hull.

variants.name: Tek Far 78 - Osk (Infested)
variants.description: This carrier is equipped with docking clamps for up to nine fighters. When loaded with a full complement of the powerful Kor Sestor robotic fighters, it is a formidable opponent.

variants.name: Aberrant Latte (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to be as close to the baseline as can be expected from such a strange creature.

variants.name: Aberrant Chomper (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to be much more heavily armed than most others of its kind.

variants.name: Aberrant Pileup (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to be somewhat larger and sturdier than others.

variants.name: Aberrant Hugger (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to be lighter and capable of higher speeds than most other Aberrant ships, yet it still carries an enlarged armament.

variants.name: Aberrant Longfellow (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to have grown extra thrusters around a lean, sleek fuselage.

variants.name: Aberrant Dancer (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular appears to have several hardpoints designed to mount turrets.

variants.name: Aberrant Junior (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to be compact, smaller-than-average, and still in the process of growing.

variants.name: Aberrant Icebreaker (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular seems to be in the process of growing more elongated, and has a tougher hull than most other variants.

variants.name: Aberrant Pike (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular has grown into a sleek, elongated shape.

variants.name: Aberrant Mole (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant has grown a sharp, bladed prow capable of fitting more forward weapons.

variants.name: Aberrant Whiskers (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant in particular appears to be growing some sort of tail-like structure, increasing its agility.

variants.name: Aberrant Trip (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. Something appears to have gone terribly wrong in the germination of this variant. It is a lopsided, deformed mass of malformed material, which survives only through sheer luck.

variants.name: Aberrant Triplet (Infested)
variants.description: This appears to be some kind of organo-metallic entity, made from a strange mosaic of organic tissue, conventional material, and an exotic matter that defies conventional analysis. This variant seems to be a composite of two or three individuals, fused together into a single mass.

variants.name: Gull (Scrapyard Rescue)
variants.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Starling (Scrapyard Rescue)
variants.description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Peregrine (Scrapyard Derelict)
variants.description: The Peregrine is the first capital warship designed by Taely using fresh insights garnered from recent experimentation as well as renewed studies of the void sprites. Encompassing over a century of development since the last generation of warships was built, it is the forerunner of a new breed of Remnant ships. 	The Peregrine was designed along the lines of old-earth's fast-attack cruisers. Drawing on inspiration from its namesake, famed for being the fastest self-propelled lifeform on Earth, it is designed to strike hard and fast. It closes quickly with enemies to unleash devastating barrages in the opening seconds of combat. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Peregrine's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Quarg Wardragon (Blue Wardragon)
variants.description: The Wardragon is used by the Quarg as a light warship. It is an older design within the Quarg's fleets, supposedly less intimidating compared to their larger warships and thus more suitable for dealings with particularly nascent species.

variants.name: Korath World-Ship (Scrapyard World-Ship (Crippled))
variants.description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

variants.name: Quarg Drake (Scrapyard Surveillance)
variants.description: The Drake is an older ship used by the Quarg for reconnaissance and initial contact with emerging spacefaring species. A popular legend states that a Drake surveyed Sol while humans began to discover spaceflight.

variants.name: Quarg Skylark (Surveillance)
variants.description: The Skylark is used by the Quarg as a freighter and warship.

variants.name: Quarg Drake (Quarg Wardragon (Surveillance))
variants.description: The Drake is an older ship used by the Quarg for reconnaissance and initial contact with emerging spacefaring species. A popular legend states that a Drake surveyed Sol while humans began to discover spaceflight.

variants.name: Grakerat 572 (Scar)
variants.description: The Sestor deployed these expeditionary vessels centuries ago to extend their reach beyond Korath space. If one were to find a suitable planet to establish a base of operations on, the Sestor could rise again.

variants.name: Leviathan (Hai Limit Breaker)
variants.description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Leviathan has been in service since long before the Republic existed. Although considered today to be an outdated and clunky model, it is still a force to be reckoned with.

variants.name: Mule (Hai Limit Breaker)
variants.description: The Mule is as much of a hodgepodge as its looks suggest. The Lionheart ship designers combined a good deal of cargo space with a decent amount of weaponry and even a fighter bay, and ended up with a ship that is mostly used as a freighter but can also be used as a warship because of its heavy armament and shields. This hodgepodge nature has its downside, though, namely its high crew requirement and the fact that any dedicated ship of the same cost will do better in a specialized role than the Mule will.

variants.name: Firebird (Hai Limit Breaker)
variants.description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Firebird is a warship with a long and storied past; Firebirds fought on both sides of the Alpha Wars over half a millennium ago, and although the equipment and systems have been upgraded continuously since then, the basic design remains almost the same. The Firebird is also a very rugged ship and easy to repair; supposedly a few of the Firebirds that fought in those wars are still in service today.

variants.name: Corvette (Hai Limit Breaker)
variants.description: Incorporating new, experimental lightweight materials, the Lionheart Corvette combines the speed and agility of a fighter with the armament of a heavy warship. Its weak hull is more than compensated for by its advanced shielding matrix.

variants.name: Headhunter (Hai Limit Breaker)
variants.description: The Headhunter is one of the newest ships designed by Lionheart: a versatile ship that can serve equally well for carrying cargo or for bounty hunting. As with many of Lionheart's designs, the hull is mostly composite fiber rather than metal, sacrificing armor strength for the sake of lighter weight and faster movement.

variants.name: Palavret Tek (Korath Raider (Scrapyard))
variants.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships.

variants.name: Palavret Tek (Korath Scrapper (shields))
variants.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships.

variants.name: Palavret Tek (Korath Scrapper (weapons))
variants.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships.

variants.name: Palavret Tek (Korath Scrapper (engines))
variants.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships.

variants.name: Palavret Tek (Korath Scrapper (carrier))
variants.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships.

variants.name: Rano'erek Tek (Korath Dredger (Scrapper))
variants.description: Korath Scrappers who scavenge in the Scrapyard are using the technology they find to customize their ships. 	This Rano'erek Tek has enhanced shield strength and hull durability. Upgraded automation in its operations keeps the ships crew requirements low.

variants.name: Rano'erek Ik (Korath Dredger (Scrapyard))
variants.description: The dangers of scavenging in the Scrapyard has lead the captain of this Rano'erek Ik to make some modifications to its hull with a focus on strengthening the shields and expanding weapons capacity.

variants.name: Mechanized Mine (SD)
variants.description: An automated mine designed to create more mines. The Mereti developed this for deployment in distant systems controlled by hostile forces.

variants.name: Model 512 (Carrier)
variants.description: This is the final state in the evolution of a Kor Mereti war drone: a perfect ring, with incredibly powerful shields and turrets firing in every direction.

variants.name: Model 256 (Carrier)
variants.description: There is a saying among the Quarg that every species which insists on studying war will eventually construct a weapon that outlives its creators. For the Korath, their war drones have very nearly done so.

variants.name: Model 128 (Carrier)
variants.description: As Kor Mereti drones continue to grow and develop into their final form, they split down the middle. At the same time, their shielding and weaponry continue to become stronger.

variants.name: Far Lek 14 (scrap)
variants.description: At the peak of their recent civil war, the Kor Sestor faction developed these automated attack drones, easy to manufacture and encased in an ultra-dense hull that can absorb a significant amount of damage. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

variants.name: Far Osk 27 (scrap)
variants.description: Because it is piloted by a computer and has no need for a cockpit or life support systems, the FS27 fighter is able to support a thicker hull, more shields, and more powerful guns than any comparable human ship. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Met Par Tek 53 (scrapper)
variants.description: Although this is the smallest Kor Sestor ship other than their fighters, it is larger than most human capital ships and considerably more powerful.

variants.name: Met Par Tek 53 (scrap) A
variants.description: Although this is the smallest Kor Sestor ship other than their fighters, it is larger than most human capital ships and considerably more powerful.

variants.name: Met Par Tek 53 (scrap) B
variants.description: Although this is the smallest Kor Sestor ship other than their fighters, it is larger than most human capital ships and considerably more powerful.

variants.name: Met Par Tek 53 (scrap) C
variants.description: Although this is the smallest Kor Sestor ship other than their fighters, it is larger than most human capital ships and considerably more powerful.

variants.name: Tek Far 109 (scrapper)
variants.description: The TF109 is designed almost solely for the purpose of carrying a fleet of Kor Sestor fighters and drones. Without them to serve as a protective screen, the ship itself is relatively helpless.

variants.name: Tek Far 109 (scrap) A
variants.description: The TF109 is designed almost solely for the purpose of carrying a fleet of Kor Sestor fighters and drones. Without them to serve as a protective screen, the ship itself is relatively helpless.

variants.name: Tek Far 109 (scrap) B
variants.description: The TF109 is designed almost solely for the purpose of carrying a fleet of Kor Sestor fighters and drones. Without them to serve as a protective screen, the ship itself is relatively helpless.

variants.name: Tek Far 109 (scrap) C
variants.description: The TF109 is designed almost solely for the purpose of carrying a fleet of Kor Sestor fighters and drones. Without them to serve as a protective screen, the ship itself is relatively helpless.

variants.name: Tek Far 78 - Osk (scrapper)
variants.description: This carrier is equipped with docking clamps for up to nine fighters. When loaded with a full complement of the powerful Kor Sestor robotic fighters, it is a formidable opponent.

variants.name: Tek Far 78 - Osk (scrap) A
variants.description: This carrier is equipped with docking clamps for up to nine fighters. When loaded with a full complement of the powerful Kor Sestor robotic fighters, it is a formidable opponent.

variants.description: The Kor Sestor war fleets rely heavily on small, powerful fighters and drones. This ship can carry up to ten of their drones, clamped to the exterior of its hull.

variants.description: The largest and deadliest of the Kor Sestor robotic warships, a single one of these battlecruisers can hold its own against an entire fleet of lesser ships.

variants.name: Kar Ik Vot 349 (scrap) A

variants.name: Kar Ik Vot 349 (scrap) C
variants.description: The largest and deadliest of the Kor Sestor robotic warships, a single one of these battlecruisers can hold its own against an entire fleet of lesser ships.

variants.name: Model 8 (scrapper)
variants.description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

variants.name: Model 8 (scrap) A
variants.description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

variants.name: Model 8 (scrap) B
variants.description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

variants.name: Model 8 (scrap) C
variants.description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

variants.name: Model 16 (scrapper)
variants.description: The Kor Mereti war drones are constructed in a modular fashion, which allows them to grow over time as more materials become available. This one is recently built, and relatively small.

variants.name: Model 16 (scrap) A
variants.description: The Kor Mereti war drones are constructed in a modular fashion, which allows them to grow over time as more materials become available. This one is recently built, and relatively small.

variants.name: Model 16 (scrap) B
variants.description: The Kor Mereti war drones are constructed in a modular fashion, which allows them to grow over time as more materials become available. This one is recently built, and relatively small.

variants.name: Model 16 (scrap) C
variants.description: The Kor Mereti war drones are constructed in a modular fashion, which allows them to grow over time as more materials become available. This one is recently built, and relatively small.

variants.name: Model 32 (scrapper)
variants.description: The Kor Mereti built their drones to mimic life: capable of growth and development throughout their life cycles. Their automated factories can convert the drones from one configuration to another by adding or removing modules.

variants.name: Model 32 (scrap) A
variants.description: The Kor Mereti built their drones to mimic life: capable of growth and development throughout their life cycles. Their automated factories can convert the drones from one configuration to another by adding or removing modules.

variants.name: Model 32 (scrap) B
variants.description: The Kor Mereti built their drones to mimic life: capable of growth and development throughout their life cycles. Their automated factories can convert the drones from one configuration to another by adding or removing modules.

variants.name: Model 32 (scrap) C
variants.description: The Kor Mereti built their drones to mimic life: capable of growth and development throughout their life cycles. Their automated factories can convert the drones from one configuration to another by adding or removing modules.

variants.name: Model 64 (scrapper)
variants.description: This medium-sized drone serves the Kor Mereti fleets in much the same way as a human Frigate, but in terms of total firepower it is far beyond anything humans have constructed.

variants.name: Model 64 (scrap) A
variants.description: This medium-sized drone serves the Kor Mereti fleets in much the same way as a human Frigate, but in terms of total firepower it is far beyond anything humans have constructed.

variants.description: This is the final state in the evolution of a Kor Mereti war drone: a perfect ring, with incredibly powerful shields and turrets firing in every direction.

variants.name: Tercheldar 150 (Tormentor)
variants.description: The Tercheldar 150 was a warship designed to fight in Mereti mine fields. It was eventually replaced by the more powerful Kar Ik Vot 349.

variants.name: Stakoshtar 187(SD)
variants.description: During their civil war, the Korath used this weapon against planetary targets. Scars from such battles still cover the surface of many of their worlds.

Missions: Scrapyard Ae Il B-11 Expeditionary Fleet

variants.name: Korath World-Ship (Fireswarm)
variants.description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

variants.name: Korath World-Ship B (Fireswarm)
variants.description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

variants.name: Korath World-Ship C (Fireswarm)
variants.description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

variants.name: Echo-Galleon (Fireswarm)
variants.description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis, including this large Efreti vessel. Facing very different challenges, and taking some inspiration from their Quarg protectors, the Efreti descendant of the "World-Ships" of old is lighter, faster, and more efficient, but less powerful than its Exile counterparts.

variants.name: Arch-Carrack (Fireswarm)
variants.description: Although they do not effectively provide for their own defense, the Efreti remain responsible for the day-to-day needs of civilian and cargo transportation. While not as bulky or powerful as other Korath designs, this freighter is much faster at making runs back and forth between the Efreti's handful of remaining planets, while still holding enough defensive loadout to ward off the odd Mereti drone that slips past the Quarg.

variants.name: Charm-Shallop (Fireswarm)
variants.description: A recent design, this Efreti multi-role vessel is found taking up many jobs previously relegated to a now scrapped fleet of early-model raiders. While some are used primarily for passenger transport, others are used in local mining to satiate the Efreti's perpetual demand for ores. Incorporating Quarg-inspired structural elements, its light frame allows it to complete tasks more nimbly than any other independent Korath ship.

variants.name: Charm-Shallop (Scanner)
variants.description: A recent design, this Efreti multi-role vessel is found taking up many jobs previously relegated to a now scrapped fleet of early-model raiders. While some are used primarily for passenger transport, others are used in local mining to satiate the Efreti's perpetual demand for ores. Incorporating Quarg-inspired structural elements, its light frame allows it to complete tasks more nimbly than any other independent Korath ship.

Missions: Scrapyard: Escort to Wanderer Worlds A
Scrapyard: Escort to Wanderer Worlds B

variants.name: Charm-Shallop (Fireswarm Scanner)
variants.description: A recent design, this Efreti multi-role vessel is found taking up many jobs previously relegated to a now scrapped fleet of early-model raiders. While some are used primarily for passenger transport, others are used in local mining to satiate the Efreti's perpetual demand for ores. Incorporating Quarg-inspired structural elements, its light frame allows it to complete tasks more nimbly than any other independent Korath ship.

variants.name: Vault-Barge (Korath Transport)
variants.description: A precursor to the Korath World-Ship. As more of their people resettle onto their former worlds, there will be a demand for smaller ships. In anticpation of this, the Kor Efret shipyards have begun reproducing these smaller freighters.

variants.name: Bravo-Monitor (Korath Transport B)
variants.description: A precursor to the Korath World-Ship. As more of their people resettle onto their former worlds, there will be a demand for smaller ships. In anticpation of this, the Kor Efret shipyards have begun reproducing these smaller patrol ships.

variants.name: Delta-Ferry (Korath Transport C)
variants.description: A precursor to the Korath World-Ship. As more of their people resettle onto their former worlds, there will be a demand for smaller ships. In anticpation of this, the Kor Efret shipyards have begun reproducing these smaller transports.

variants.name: Korath Ranger (Fireswarm)
variants.description: The Threshold-Destroyer was the first warship deployed at the start of the Korath civil war that eventually brought the downfall of their civilization. With the recent Blight Shadow attack on one of their World-ships, the Kor Efret are starting to reproduce these warships as escorts.

variants.name: Korath Ranger (Grappler)
variants.description: The Threshold-Destroyer was the first warship deployed at the start of the Korath civil war that eventually brought the downfall of their civilization. With the recent Blight Shadow attack on one of their World-ships, the Kor Efret are starting to reproduce these warships as escorts.

Missions: Midnight Expansion: Kor Efret Tevah-Ark Coracle

variants.name: Shelf-Sloop (Thermal Scorcher)
variants.description: The Kor Efret have redesigned an old warship for more peaceful pursuits. The Shelf-Sloop is able to install larger engines than its predecessor while also accomodating a turret. Its weapons options, however, are more limited.

variants.name: Korath Devourer (alternate)
variants.description: Some Korath scrappers are more interested in harvesting raw materials rather than technology. The Devar'ur is their attempt to streamline the collection process. The Korath Incinerator is integrated into the ship and cannot be removed.

variants.name: Kasichara A'awoj (Jump)
variants.description: Originally designed as a luxury habitat for those wealthy enough to escape living on the polluted surface of Korath worlds, when the Korath were exiled, it was into these gargantuan "world-ships" they first crammed themselves into and fled to the core on. Nostalgically, the class was then renamed after the devastated and now forbidden Korath homeworld in honor of the Korsmanathi, ancestors of all Korath. 	Since the introduction of purpose-built city ships, these ancient vessels have mostly been decommissioned, turned into stations, or reconfigured into smaller, specialized vessels. Yet a few still ply the starlanes, crewed by those too restless to live on stations yet not bold enough to go on raids.

variants.name: Model 512 (Jump)
variants.description: This is the final state in the evolution of a Kor Mereti war drone: a perfect ring, with incredibly powerful shields and turrets firing in every direction.

variants.name: Kar Ik Vot 349 (Jump)
variants.description: The largest and deadliest of the Kor Sestor robotic warships, a single one of these battlecruisers can hold its own against an entire fleet of lesser ships.

variants.name: Far Osk 27 (Flamethrower)
variants.description: Because it is piloted by a computer and has no need for a cockpit or life support systems, the FS27 fighter is able to support a thicker hull, more shields, and more powerful guns than any comparable human ship. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Korath Explorer (Exile)
variants.description: Korath Explorers were originally made to escape the harsh gravitional forces of Sagittarius A*. It is a science vessel designed for gathering data and resources.

variants.name: Korath Explorer (Sestor)
variants.description: Korath Explorers were originally made to escape the harsh gravitional forces of Sagittarius A*. It is a science vessel designed for gathering data and resources.

variants.name: Korath Explorer (Banisher)
variants.description: Korath Explorers were originally made to escape the harsh gravitional forces of Sagittarius A*. It is a science vessel designed for gathering data and resources.

variants.name: Korath Explorer (Grab-Strike)
variants.description: Korath Explorers were originally made to escape the harsh gravitional forces of Sagittarius A*. It is a science vessel designed for gathering data and resources.

variants.name: 'olofez (Korath Chaser (Miner))
variants.description: The 'olofez-Class Carried Chaser, named for a renowned artist who painted their visions of ancient hunts, is a fighter carried by most Exile capital ships. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Korath World-Ship D (Crippled)
variants.description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

variants.name: Korath Ranger (Korath Warmonger)
variants.description: The Threshold-Destroyer was the first warship deployed at the start of the Korath civil war that eventually brought the downfall of their civilization. With the recent Blight Shadow attack on one of their World-ships, the Kor Efret are starting to reproduce these warships as escorts.

variants.name: Korath Ranger (Korath Warmonger (Fireswarm))
variants.description: The Threshold-Destroyer was the first warship deployed at the start of the Korath civil war that eventually brought the downfall of their civilization. With the recent Blight Shadow attack on one of their World-ships, the Kor Efret are starting to reproduce these warships as escorts.

variants.name: Korath Ranger (Korath Warmonger (Grappler))
variants.description: The Threshold-Destroyer was the first warship deployed at the start of the Korath civil war that eventually brought the downfall of their civilization. With the recent Blight Shadow attack on one of their World-ships, the Kor Efret are starting to reproduce these warships as escorts.

outfits.name: Scrap Metal
outfits.description: Twisted wreckage from a destroyed spacecraft or other artificial object.

outfits.name: Anomalous Husk
outfits.description: Trace amounts of dessicated biological residue are attached to this mass of glassy material. It appears to be well suited for absorbing heat, but otherwise does not appear to possess be any unusual physical properties.

outfits.name: Blood Lightning?
outfits.description: The red lightning generated from this organ will slightly recharge the batteries of a ship struck by it. This is accompanied by a weapon jamming effect similar to, but different from, ionization.

outfits.name: Korath Fireswarm Access Temporary License
outfits.description: This technical manual details how to manufacture Fireswarm Missiles. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer Fireswarm Missiles even if it is normally not stocked there. 	The version available for purchase is written in an alien language you don't understand... 	This tech can be researched instead of purchased. (Hover/Tap the attributes below for more details.) 	After this is acquired it can be found in the Licenses area when at the outfitter.

outfits.name: Korath Fireswarm Tech License
outfits.description: This technical manual details how to manufacture Korath Fireswarms. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer Korath Fireswarms even if it is normally not stocked there.

outfits.name: Automine Deployer
outfits.description: A newly developed Mereti weapon, the Automine Deployer forgoes the need to carry ammunition. Instead, the mines are constructed using Korath nanomachine technology. These weapons were developed exclusively for use by the Mechanized Mine automata.

outfits.name: Burrower Mining Beam
outfits.description: This classic Korath mining laser lacks the shield disruption effect found in its modern counterpart, but is more effective for its intended purpose.

outfits.name: Korath Eliminator
outfits.description: The World Slicer is an orbital strike weapon that can carve deep into a planet's crust. Many worlds were destroyed in the Korath civil war by these tools of destruction.

outfits.name: Korath Fireswarm Nest
outfits.description: These storage shells hold additional Fireswarm Missiles for the Fireswarm Launcher. 	Each Fireswarm Nest includes a power charger to keep stored Fireswarms ready for launch. They also plug into your ships power systems, allowing surplus energy to be used elsewhere.

outfits.name: Korath Fireswarm Depot
outfits.description: The Fireswarm Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Fireswarm Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to oversee maintenance of the ammunition.

outfits.name: Korath Fireswarm
outfits.description: Each Fireswarm Missile carries ten smaller missiles. When not being fired as weapons, they add to the energy storage capacity of your ship.

outfits.name: Korath Fireswarm Launcher
outfits.description: The Fireswarm Missile is a cluster munition that carries a swarm of smaller missiles which can overwhelm slow defense systems. 	If multiple Fireswarm Launchers are installed, they will all fire simultaneously.

outfits.name: Korath Grappler
outfits.description: Grappler Gun projectiles are designed to pull a target closer to the Korath ship, to keep it from escaping.

outfits.name: Korath Incinerator
outfits.description: A giant smelter. Ships and asteroids go in. Melted metal comes out.

outfits.name: Korath Scattermine
outfits.description: The Scattermine Deployer is a crude device for deploying a cloud of stationary mines along the ship's wake. One drawback of deploying the mines in this manner is that premature detonation can damage the ship dropping the mines.

outfits.name: Korath Tractor
outfits.description: The Salvager Turret is used to tow derelict ships away from dangerous battlefields.

outfits.name: Korath Tormentor
outfits.description: An ancient Korath design, the Tormentor lacks the burst fire and synchronization features which were later implemented in the Repeater.

outfits.name: Tormentor Turret
outfits.description: Tormentor Turrets only have a single Tormentor mounted on their base, but have low space requirements for installation.

outfits.name: Hyperdrive (Damaged)
outfits.description: A malfunction is causing this hyperdrive to consume triple the amount of fuel that a normal hyperdrive would.

outfits.name: Obsolete Transceiver
outfits.description: This is a miniaturized communication device, which can be connected to all the major systems of a Kor Sestor automaton, apparently as a way of controlling them remotely. This appears to be an older model transceiver incompatible with the encryption and authentication methods utilized by modern Kor Sestor.

outfits.name: Obsolete Transmitter
outfits.description: This is some sort of advanced, miniature computer combined with various sensor inputs. Presumably, in some way it controls nearby Sestor automata when communications over a hyperspace communications network are unavailable. This device is designed to interface with older model transceivers and is incompatible with the encryption and authentication methods utilized by modern Kor Sestor.

outfits.name: Triple Plasma Core (Damaged)
outfits.description: While there is no visible damage, you are certain this unit is malfunctioning. The power output is no better than a Double Plasma Core.

outfits.name: Afterburner (Meteor Class)
outfits.description: This older afterburner design was once a common sight in Korath space. 	Korath afterburners pump super-heated hyperspace fuel directly into a thruster's plasma stream, resulting in a phenomenal explosion that nearly melts the thruster.

outfits.name: Arkrof GP Hybrid Reverser
outfits.description: Efreti engineers have adapted their smallest gravitoplasma-based thrusters into this reverser. 	This Korath engine has been modified to be partially installed in a ship's weapon space, allowing it to push the ship in reverse.

outfits.name: Farves GP Hybrid Reverser
outfits.description: Reversers aren't particularly popular among the Kor Efret, yet Efreti factories still produce them for a niche market. 	This Korath engine has been modified to be partially installed in a ship's weapon space, allowing it to push the ship in reverse.

outfits.name: Gaktem GP Hybrid Reverser
outfits.description: Efreti reverse thrusters are just as powerful as their normal gravitoplasma thrusters. 	This Korath engine has been modified to be partially installed in a ship's weapon space, allowing it to push the ship in reverse.

outfits.name: Nelmeb GP Hybrid Reverser
outfits.description: These are the largest gravitoplasma reversers that the Efreti have to offer. 	This Korath engine has been modified to be partially installed in a ship's weapon space, allowing it to push the ship in reverse.

outfits.name: Steering (Planetary Class)(Damaged)
outfits.description: A malfunction in the steering system has reduced its effectiveness significantly.

outfits.name: Thruster (Planetary Class)(Damaged)
outfits.description: Damage to the thrusters has reduced output by half.

outfits.name: Blight Rifle
outfits.description: A prototype the Remnant created using Blight Shadow eggs. This weapon drains energy from whatever it is used on. This can disrupt electronic door locks and reduces living creatures into withered husks. This effect also replenishes its own energy, making it an exceptional weapon for long sieges. Death from this weapon appears to be cruel and painful. If the governments back home knew of its existence, it would probably be banned.

outfits.name: Korath Sentinel
outfits.description: A semi autonomous robot made with Sestor technology, these defense droids patrol high security areas on the ships of experienced Kor scrapper crews.

outfits.name: Slicer Torch
outfits.description: An improvised weapon made by a Scrapyard hermit. It easily and quickly cuts through ship bulkheads, making it an ideal tool for raiding ships. It can also be used to make emergency escape routes when one is seemingly trapped in a dead end. This weapon can get incredibly hot after prolonged use, so it may be unwise to use it for defending against extended sieges.

outfits.name: Blight Shadow Eggs
outfits.description: Alien eggs found in a derelict space station. Might be worth something to the right person.

outfits.name: Node 8 Jammer
outfits.description: This resembles a clump of Kor Mereti Reasoning Nodes, but is an obvious fake when compared to the real thing. Despite this, it's a sophisticated piece of technology with the ability to disrupt communications on a network it's installed on.

outfits.name: Node 8 Processor
outfits.description: This resembles a clump of Kor Mereti Reasoning Nodes. It must be related to the Mereti communications network somehow.

outfits.name: Efret Salvage License
outfits.description: Gives you access to salvaged outfits from the Kor Efret. You're not sure if this is a formal license or simply a means to communicate to the outfitter that you're looking for used parts...

outfits.name: Korath Afterburner
outfits.description: Korath Scrappers recovered his small afterburner from the ruins of their ancestral space. 	Korath afterburners pump super-heated hyperspace fuel directly into a thruster's plasma stream, resulting in a phenomenal explosion that nearly melts the thruster.

outfits.name: Korath Smelter
outfits.description: A giant smelter. Ships go in. Melted metal comes out.
